binaries.title: mup.sys - IoStatus.Information length rounding in UNC hardening prefix-table enumeration (FSCTL 0x100038)

changed_functions.name: DriverEntry
key_changes: Patched DriverEntry removes the wil_InitializeFeatureStaging and wil_UninitializeFeatureStaging calls; the associated wil_details_* helper routines are dropped from the patched image (WIL feature-staging teardown)
WPP trace-GUID symbol references change (WPP_db358c5e599b3a76078eb4333eba59a2_Traceguids -> WPP_6f54a748a3c5368360ebb9a9c7e63214_Traceguids) and address relocations from the image-layout shift
No change to MUP request handling
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-staging-library teardown plus WPP relocation. Not security-relevant.

changed_functions.name: MupiPackUncHardeningPrefixTableForFsctl
key_changes: On the STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW (0x80000005) top-of-loop path (aligned next-record offset >= buffer size with a prior record written): patched adds `mov edi, esi` at 0x1C001AB50 to report the 16-byte-aligned offset; unpatched jumps to exit leaving edi at the end offset of the last written record (unaligned)

On the (rdi+0xF) integer-overflow path: patched sets edi = 0xFFFFFFFF at 0x1C001AB59 before returning STATUS_INVALID_BUFFER_SIZE (0xC0000095); unpatched leaves edi stale on this path. The caller does not propagate a 0xC0000095 result to IoStatus.Information, so this difference is not observable through the caller

Process-path restructuring: unpatched commits edi=aligned before packing (0x1C001AE01) and uses rsi as the write pointer; patched uses rbp for the write pointer and commits edi after packing. Functionally equivalent on the success path
Register reallocation (bpl->r15b success flag, r15b->r13b buffer_too_small flag, r13->rsi arg4 save): cosmetic
changed_functions.security_notes: MupiPackUncHardeningPrefixTableForFsctl enumerates registered UNC providers from the MUP Unicode prefix table and copies each record into a METHOD_BUFFERED output buffer. A running byte offset (edi) is returned via *arg4, which the caller MupFsctlGetUncHardeningPrefixTable stores into IRP->IoStatus.Information. The two build-differences change the reported offset on non-success exit paths. This is NOT a security fix, for three independently sufficient reasons: (1) the caller zero-fills the entire OutputBufferLength-sized system buffer via memset before population in BOTH builds (unpatched 0x1C0019AE9, patched 0x1C00197B9), so no uninitialized pool data is ever present to disclose; (2) METHOD_BUFFERED completion copies min(IoStatus.Information, OutputBufferLength) bytes from the OutputBufferLength-sized buffer - the unpatched stale value on the STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW path is the end offset of the last written record, which is < OutputBufferLength, so it under-reports and never causes an out-of-bounds read; the patched aligned value is >= buffer size on that branch and is clamped down by the I/O Manager; (3) the caller only writes IoStatus.Information for results >= 0, == 0x80000005, or == 0xC0000023 (unpatched 0x1C0019AFD-0x1C0019B13), so the integer-overflow (0xC0000095) length difference is discarded. Net observable effect: the reported bytesReturned differs by up to the 16-byte alignment padding between builds; the returned bytes are identical (zero-filled tail). No information disclosure, no memory corruption, no out-of-bounds access.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED MupiPackUncHardeningPrefixTableForFsctl @ 0x1C001AD4C (key basic blocks):
; top of loop
0x1C001ADD2  lea     eax, [rdi+0Fh]          ; aligned candidate = off + 15
0x1C001ADD5  cmp     eax, edi                ; wrap check
0x1C001ADD7  jb      loc_1C001AE8F           ; wrap -> STATUS_INVALID_BUFFER_SIZE (edi not forced to -1)
0x1C001ADDD  and     eax, 0FFFFFFF0h         ; align to 16
0x1C001ADE0  cmp     eax, r12d               ; aligned vs buffer size
0x1C001ADE3  jb      short loc_1C001ADF7     ; fits -> process
0x1C001ADE5  test    bpl, bpl                ; prior success?
0x1C001ADE8  jnz     loc_1C001AE7E           ; yes -> STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW
; process path (eager commit of off):
0x1C001ADF7  mov     esi, eax
0x1C001ADFC  add     rsi, [rsp+58h+arg_10]
0x1C001AE01  mov     edi, eax                ; off = aligned (committed here)
0x1C001AE03  sub     edx, eax
; STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW exit (edi unchanged):
0x1C001AE7E  mov     ebx, 80000005h
0x1C001AE83  jmp     short loc_1C001AE94
; integer-overflow exit:
0x1C001AE8F  mov     ebx, 0C0000095h
; final write:
0x1C001AEC6  mov     [r13+0], edi            ; *arg4 = off

PATCHED MupiPackUncHardeningPrefixTableForFsctl @ 0x1C001AA1C (key basic blocks):
; top of loop
0x1C001AAA3  lea     esi, [rdi+0Fh]
0x1C001AAA6  cmp     esi, edi
0x1C001AAA8  jb      loc_1C001AB59           ; wrap -> handler that forces off = -1
0x1C001AAAE  and     esi, 0FFFFFFF0h
0x1C001AAB1  cmp     esi, r12d
0x1C001AAB4  jb      short loc_1C001AAC8
0x1C001AAB6  test    r15b, r15b
0x1C001AAB9  jnz     loc_1C001AB4B           ; STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW
; process path (rbp write pointer, off deferred):
0x1C001AAC8  mov     ebp, esi
0x1C001AACD  add     rbp, [rsp+58h+arg_10]
0x1C001AAD2  sub     edx, esi
; STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW exit (off = aligned):
0x1C001AB4B  mov     ebx, 80000005h
0x1C001AB50  mov     edi, esi
0x1C001AB52  jmp     short loc_1C001AB63
; integer-overflow exit (off = -1):
0x1C001AB59  mov     edi, 0FFFFFFFFh
0x1C001AB5E  mov     ebx, 0C0000095h
; final write:
0x1C001AB9A  mov     [rsi], edi              ; *arg4 = off

security_findings.entry_point: FSCTL 0x100038 (FILE_DEVICE_MULTI_UNC_PROVIDER device 0x10, Function 0x0E, METHOD_BUFFERED) via IRP_MJ_FILE_SYSTEM_CONTROL on \Device\Mup
call_chain: MupFsControl @ 0x1C000DE40 (FSCTL switch on Parameters.DeviceIoControl code; case 0x100038)
MupFsctlGetUncHardeningPrefixTable @ 0x1C0019ABC unpatched / 0x1C001978C patched (memset(SystemBuffer,0,OutputBufferLength); writes returned offset to IRP->IoStatus.Information at IRP+0x38)
MupiPackUncHardeningPrefixTableForFsctl @ 0x1C001AD4C unpatched / 0x1C001AA1C patched (enumerates UNC providers from prefix table)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: none (output-length reporting correctness; no reachable security impact)
security_findings.description: MupiPackUncHardeningPrefixTableForFsctl walks the MUP Unicode prefix table of registered UNC providers and copies each provider's record into a METHOD_BUFFERED output buffer. A running byte offset (edi) is returned via *arg4 and stored by the caller into IRP->IoStatus.Information. The patched build differs on two non-success exit paths: on STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW (0x80000005) it reports the 16-byte-aligned offset (mov edi, esi at 0x1C001AB50) instead of the unpatched end offset of the last written record; on the (rdi+0xF) integer-overflow path it forces edi = 0xFFFFFFFF (0x1C001AB59). Neither difference is security-relevant: the caller zero-fills the entire OutputBufferLength-sized system buffer before population in both builds (unpatched 0x1C0019AE9, patched 0x1C00197B9), so no uninitialized data can be disclosed; the METHOD_BUFFERED copy is clamped to min(IoStatus.Information, OutputBufferLength) so no out-of-bounds read occurs; and the caller only propagates IoStatus.Information for results >= 0, 0x80000005, or 0xC0000023, discarding the 0xC0000095 path. The only observable effect is that bytesReturned on the STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW case differs by up to the 16-byte alignment padding; the returned bytes (zero-filled tail) are identical.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. Open a handle to \Device\Mup (or a UNC path) from user mode. 2. Issue FSCTL 0x100038 with an OutputBufferLength large enough for the first provider record but too small for the aligned second record. 3. The system must have at least 2 registered UNC providers. 4. The function returns STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW (0x80000005) and reports an unaligned bytesReturned on the unpatched build vs a 16-byte-aligned value on the patched build. The returned bytes are identical zero-filled data in both builds.
security_findings.primitive: None. The output buffer is zero-filled by the caller in both builds, so there is no uninitialized-memory disclosure. The METHOD_BUFFERED completion copies min(IoStatus.Information, OutputBufferLength) bytes from the OutputBufferLength-sized system buffer, so there is no out-of-bounds read. The unpatched stale value on the STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW path is < OutputBufferLength (under-reports). The affected register is only used as the reported byte count and drives no write. Net impact: a reported-length rounding difference with no security consequence.
security_findings.evidence:
// Caller MupFsctlGetUncHardeningPrefixTable @ 0x1C0019ABC (unpatched) zero-fills the whole buffer:
0x1C0019AE3  mov     ebx, [rax+8]           ; ebx = OutputBufferLength
0x1C0019AE6  mov     r8d, ebx               ; Size
0x1C0019AE9  call    memset                 ; memset(SystemBuffer, 0, OutputBufferLength)
0x1C0019AF8  call    MupiPackUncHardeningPrefixTableForFsctl
0x1C0019AFD  test    eax, eax
0x1C0019AFF  jns     short loc_1C0019B0F
0x1C0019B01  cmp     eax, 80000005h
0x1C0019B06  jz      short loc_1C0019B0F
0x1C0019B08  cmp     eax, 0C0000023h
0x1C0019B0D  jnz     short loc_1C0019B17     ; 0xC0000095 falls here -> length NOT written
0x1C0019B0F  mov     ecx, [rsp+28h+arg_0]   ; returned offset
0x1C0019B13  mov     [rsi+38h], rcx         ; IRP->IoStatus.Information = returned offset

// UNPATCHED STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW exit (edi = end of last record, < OutputBufferLength):
0x1C001AE7E  mov     ebx, 80000005h
0x1C001AE83  jmp     short loc_1C001AE94
0x1C001AEC6  mov     [r13+0], edi

// PATCHED STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW exit (edi = aligned offset):
0x1C001AB4B  mov     ebx, 80000005h
0x1C001AB50  mov     edi, esi
0x1C001AB52  jmp     short loc_1C001AB63
0x1C001AB9A  mov     [rsi], edi
security_findings.assembly: See changed_functions[1].assembly_diff for the full key basic blocks of both builds. Full unpatched function body spans 0x1C001AD4C-0x1C001AEDE; full patched function body spans 0x1C001AA1C-0x1C001ABB0.

breakpoints.why: Entry point. arg2=edx=OutputBufferLength (from the IO stack), arg3=r8=zero-filled SystemBuffer, arg4=r9=pointer to the output offset variable. Observe the reported length differences at the exit breakpoints below.

STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW exit. edi holds the end offset of the last written record (unaligned, < OutputBufferLength). In the patched build the equivalent path sets edi = esi (aligned) at 0x1C001AB50. This is only a reported-length difference; the buffer bytes are identical zero-filled data.
Final `mov [r13], edi` writing the reported offset to *arg4. MupFsctlGetUncHardeningPrefixTable stores this into IRP->IoStatus.Information (IRP+0x38). Confirms the value propagated to user mode.

watch: edi at 0x1C001AE7E - end offset of last written record (unaligned) vs the patched aligned value; both are <= OutputBufferLength after clamping
r8 at entry = zero-filled SystemBuffer (memset by caller before the call)
The difference: unpatched reports prev_end (unaligned); patched reports ((prev_end + 0xf) & ~0xf) (aligned). Both refer to zero-filled buffer contents.
key_offsets: 0x1C0019AE9: caller memset(SystemBuffer, 0, OutputBufferLength) - zero-fills the whole buffer in both builds, eliminating any disclosure
0x1C001AE01: `mov edi, eax` - unpatched eager commit of the aligned offset in the process branch
0x1C001AE7E: `mov ebx, 80000005h` - STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW set without a preceding edi update (unpatched)
0x1C001AB50: `mov edi, esi` - patched reports the aligned offset before exit
0x1C001AB59: `mov edi, 0FFFFFFFFh` - patched forces -1 on the integer-overflow path (result 0xC0000095, discarded by caller)
0x1C0019B0D: caller `jnz loc_1C0019B17` - 0xC0000095 result skips the IoStatus.Information write
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Ensure the system has at least 2 registered UNC providers. 2. Open \Device\Mup or a UNC path. 3. Call DeviceIoControl(hDevice, 0x100038, NULL, 0, outputBuffer, smallBufferSize, &bytesReturned, NULL) where smallBufferSize fits the first record but not the aligned second record. 4. The driver returns STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW (0x80000005); bytesReturned is unaligned on the unpatched build and 16-byte-aligned on the patched build. The returned bytes (zero-filled tail) are identical.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: At 0x1C001AE7E, edi is an unaligned value (end of the last record). At 0x1C001AEC6 that value is written to the output and becomes IRP->IoStatus.Information. In the patched build the equivalent exit reports the aligned value. The difference is at most the 16-byte alignment padding; no additional or different data is returned.
debugging_aids.notes: MUP (Multiple UNC Provider) handles UNC path resolution and provider enumeration. FSCTL 0x100038 is METHOD_BUFFERED (DeviceType 0x10 FILE_DEVICE_MULTI_UNC_PROVIDER, Function 0x0E). The output buffer holds per-provider records aligned to 16-byte boundaries. Because the caller zero-fills the buffer and the I/O Manager clamps the METHOD_BUFFERED copy to OutputBufferLength, the reported-length difference between builds has no security consequence.

summary: No security-relevant change. The patched MupiPackUncHardeningPrefixTableForFsctl (unpatched 0x1C001AD4C, patched 0x1C001AA1C) adjusts the running offset reported as IRP->IoStatus.Information on the STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW and integer-overflow exit paths of the UNC provider enumeration loop (reachable via FSCTL 0x100038 on \Device\Mup). The unpatched build leaves the offset at the unaligned end of the last written record on the buffer-overflow path; the patched build reports the 16-byte-aligned offset instead (and forces 0xFFFFFFFF on the integer-overflow path). This has no security impact: the caller MupFsctlGetUncHardeningPrefixTable zero-fills the entire OutputBufferLength-sized system buffer before population in both builds (memset at 0x1C0019AE9 / 0x1C00197B9), the METHOD_BUFFERED copy is clamped to OutputBufferLength (the unpatched value under-reports and never over-reads), and the integer-overflow result code (0xC0000095) is not propagated to IoStatus.Information. Separately, DriverEntry drops its WIL feature-staging init/uninit and the wil_details_* helper routines are removed from the image (staging-library teardown), which is also not security-relevant. The reported bytesReturned differs by at most the 16-byte alignment padding; the returned bytes are identical zero-filled data.
